html:
The budget proposal contains the government's proposal for the state budget for 2026, as well as other proposals and assessments that follow from the Rules of Procedure of the Parliament and the Budget Act (2011:203).

Proposals for Parliamentary Decisions

Government Proposals:

  1. The Riksdag (Swedish Parliament) adopts the proposed amendment to the Budget Act (2011:203) (sections 3.1 and 5.5).

Economic Policy Guidelines and the Draft State Budget for 2026:

  1. The Riksdag approves the guidelines for economic and budget policy (section 1).
  2. The Riksdag sets the expenditure ceiling for the state, including the pension system, at 1,936 billion Swedish kronor for 2026 and 2,009 billion Swedish kronor for 2027, due to technical adjustments and fiscal reasons (section 5.2).
  3. The Riksdag sets the expenditure ceiling for the state, including the pension system, at 2,105 billion Swedish kronor for 2028 (section 5.2).
  4. The Riksdag establishes a goal for the financial savings of the public sector, aiming for a balanced saving over the economic cycle, starting in 2027 (section 5.5).
  5. The Riksdag approves the calculation of state revenue in the 2026 budget (section 6.1 and Appendix 1, section 2).
  6. The Riksdag approves the preliminary calculation of state revenue for 2027 and 2028 as a guideline for the government's budget work (section 6.1 and Table 6.1).
  7. The Riksdag decides on the allocation of expenditure to different areas for 2026 (section 7.2 and Table 7.1).
  8. The Riksdag approves the calculation of changes in appropriation reserves for 2026 (section 7.2 and Table 7.1).
  9. The Riksdag approves the calculation of expenditure for the pension system, separate from the state budget, for 2026 (section 7.2 and Table 7.1).
  10. The Riksdag approves the preliminary allocation of expenditure to different areas for 2027 and 2028 as a guideline for the government's budget work (section 7.3 and Table 7.3).
  11. The Riksdag authorizes the government to borrow funds according to Section 5 of the Budget Act (2011:203) during 2026 (section 8.2).
  12. The Riksdag approves the calculation of the National Debt Office's net lending for 2026 (section 8.2 and Table 8.1).
  13. The Riksdag approves the calculation of the cash flow correction for 2026 (section 8.2 and Table 8.1).
  14. The Riksdag authorizes the government to decide on loans from the National Debt Office for investments in fixed assets used in state activities, totaling up to 74,900,000,000 Swedish kronor, including previous borrowings (section 10.2).
  15. The Riksdag authorizes the government to decide on credits for the interest accounts of government agencies at the National Debt Office, totaling up to 23,400,000,000 Swedish kronor, including previously utilized credits (section 10.2).
  16. The Riksdag authorizes the government, within the limitations of Section 3, Paragraph 8, second sentence, of the Budget Act (2011:203), to exceed an appropriation not intended for administrative purposes if a parliamentary decision to change the appropriation cannot be awaited, and the exceedance falls within the established expenditure ceiling for the state (section 10.7).

Proposed Laws:

  1. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Coupon Tax Act (1970:624) (sections 12.1 and 13.9).
  2. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1986:468) on the deduction of foreign tax (sections 12.2 and 13.9).
  3. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1991:586) on special income tax for non-residents (sections 12.3 and 13.3).
  4. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1991:586) on special income tax for non-residents (sections 12.4 and 13.3).
  5. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1994:1776) on energy tax (sections 12.5, 13.12–13.14).
  6. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1994:1776) on energy tax (sections 12.6 and 13.15).
  7. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1994:1776) on energy tax (sections 12.7 and 13.13).
  8. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (1994:1920) on general payroll tax (sections 12.8 and 13.7).
  9. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Income Tax Act (1999:1229) (sections 12.9, 13.2, 13.8, 13.9 and expenditure area 15, Student Support, section 3.11.4).
  10. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Social Security Contributions Act (2000:980) (sections 12.10 and 13.7).
  11. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2006:227) on traffic tax (sections 12.11 and 13.16).
  12. The Riksdag adopts the proposed law repealing the Act (2008:205) on the tax on aviation gasoline (sections 12.12 and 13.13).
  13. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2011:1200) on electricity certificates (sections 12.13 and 13.14).
  14. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Tax Procedure Act (2011:1244) (sections 12.14 and 13.8).
  15. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2019:453) on the approval of recipients of gifts for gift tax reduction (sections 12.15 and 13.9).
  16. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2023:362) amending the Act (1994:1776) on energy tax (sections 12.16 and 13.13).
  17. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2024:294) amending the Act (2006:227) on traffic tax (sections 12.17 and 13.16).
  18. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2025:539) amending the Income Tax Act (1999:1229) (sections 12.18 and 13.9).
  19. The Riksdag adopts the proposed amendment to the Act (2025:540) amending the Tax Procedure Act (2011:1244) (sections 12.19 and 13.8).
tldr: The government has presented its proposal for Sweden's budget for the year 2026. This outlines how much money the state will have available to spend and how much it will be allowed to borrow in the coming years.
lattlast:
The government proposes a plan outlining how state funds will be used during 2026, 2027, and 2028. A key decision is the so-called spending cap, which sets the maximum amount the state can spend. For 2026, the proposed limit is 1,936 billion Swedish krona, and for 2027, the proposal is 2,009 billion Swedish krona.

The plan also includes rules regarding how much money the state should save. Starting in 2027, the goal is for the state to have savings that are balanced with the economy. This is to ensure a stable economy even when the country faces difficulties.

The government also wants the authority to borrow money to pay for essential things. This includes the possibility of borrowing up to 74.9 billion Swedish krona to purchase equipment and buildings needed for government operations. There are also proposals to change various taxes, such as taxes on energy, road traffic, and income tax.

To maintain financial stability, the government also proposes that the National Debt Office, which manages the state's finances, should handle loans and credit. This means that the state has a clear plan for both how the money will be used and how it will be repaid.
